01

Syllabus

BdMSO 2026 official Mathematics and Science syllabus.

⬇ Download Detailed Syllabus 285 KB

📐 Mathematics

  • Arithmetic and Number Theory
  • Geometry and Spatial Sense
  • Algebra and Pattern Recognition
  • Data Analysis and Statistics
  • Combinatorics and Probability
  • Recreational Mathematics

🔬 Science

  • Scientific Method
  • Life Science
  • Physical Science
  • Earth and Solar System
  • Geography and Environment
  • Biotechnology
02

Participation Criteria

Who can participate in BdMSO 2026. Eligibility is strictly governed by the cutoff date of October 1, 2026.

✅ Eligibility Requirements

  • Must be 13 years old or younger
  • Born on or after October 1, 2013
  • Enrolled in Grade 6 or lower as of October 1, 2026
  • British curriculum (or similar streams) students promoted to Grade 7 in July/August 2026 are not eligible, even if they were in Grade 6 during the registration period in May

📋 Categorization

  • Olympiad: Grade 6 or below
  • Quiz: Grade 3 or below

Eligible students can participate in only one category — either the Quiz or the Olympiad.

03

Awards & Prizes

What participants and winners take home from BdMSO 2026.

Highest scorer · 1 per section

Champion Trophy

Awarded to the medalist with the highest score in each section - Mathematics, Science, and Combined - presented on the final day of the National Residential Camp.

  • Champion Trophy (Mathematics)
  • Champion Trophy (Science)
  • Champion Trophy (Combined)
Top tier

All-Round Winner

Awarded to students who place in the top 10% in both Mathematics and Science independently. The strongest performers across both subjects.

  • Medal & Certificate of Achievement
  • Priority invitation to the National Residential Camp
  • Direct path to the Team Selection Test (TST)
Subject tier

Mathematics Winner

Top 10% in Mathematics. Recognised for sustained excellence in the Maths section of the National Olympiad.

  • Medal & Certificate of Achievement
  • Invitation to the National Residential Camp
  • Eligible for the Mathematics TST
Subject tier

Science Winner

Top 10% in Science. Recognised for excellence in the Science section of the National Olympiad.

  • Medal & Certificate of Achievement
  • Invitation to the National Residential Camp
  • Eligible for the Science TST
Quiz · Class 3 or below

Quiz Winners

Top 10% winners of the BdMSO Quiz Competition receive a medal and Certificate of Achievement. The highest scorer takes home a Champion Trophy.

  • Medal & Certificate of Achievement
  • Champion Trophy (highest scorer)
  • Participant T-shirt, ID, badge, snacks & lunch
Every contestant

Certificate of Participation

Every contestant who attends the National Round of BdMSO receives a Certificate of Participation and takes home the participation pack regardless of score.

  • Participant T-shirt, ID & badge
  • Certificate of Participation
  • Sticker, snacks, & the day's cultural program
  • Lunch provided at the Dhaka & Chattogram venues

See the FAQ for eligibility, fees, and how the weighted-average team selection works.

04

Rules and Regulations

Official eligibility rules, exam conduct, and BdMSO code of conduct.

⬇ Download Detailed Regulations 738 KB
  • The Olympiad is a single two-hour combined exam covering both Mathematics and Science sections.
  • BdMSO operates as a single category for IMSO qualification. A separate Quiz Competition will be held for Grade 3 or lower as encouragement.
  • English is the official language for all examination papers, instructions, and communications.
  • Olympiad registration fees range from 1,000–1,500 BDT. National Camp fees range from 3,000–5,000 BDT. TST fee is 1,000 BDT per subject. International participation fee is announced after the TST.
05

Mark Distribution and Question Pattern

How marks are allocated for the National Olympiad and Team Selection Test (TST).

⬇ Download Detailed Breakdown 296 KB

The Olympiad is a Math + Science exam, 60 minutes per subject (Mathematics + Science, 100 marks total). The Team Selection Test follows the IMSO format: separate day-long evaluations per subject, 100 marks each.

Olympiad: BdMSO National Olympiad (60 min per subject)

SubjectSectionDistributionMarks
MathematicsShort Answer20 questions20
Essay10 questions20
Exploration2 questions10
Subtotal-50
ScienceMultiple Choice20 questions20
Short Answer10 questions20
Brief Problems2 questions10
Subtotal-50
Total100

Team Selection Test (per subject, day-long)

SubjectSectionDistributionDurationMarks
MathematicsShort Answer25 questions60 min25
Essay13 questions90 min39
Exploration6 questions120 min36
Total-100
ScienceTheory 1 (MCQ)30 questions60 min30
Theory 2 (Open Ended)9 questions90 min45
Experiment (Lab)2 questions120 min25
Total-100

07

Guide for Parents

What you need to know before your child begins.

What is BdMSO?

The official qualifying olympiad to select primary-school students to represent Bangladesh at IMSO - the International Math & Science Olympiad.

Explore Programs

From foundation courses to residential camps - browse all year-round programs to find the right fit for your child. View all programs →

Selection process

Register → National Olympiad → Residential Camp → TST → Preparatory Camp → International delegation. See the Road to IMSO.

08

Frequently Asked Questions

Who is eligible to participate in BdMSO?

BdMSO is open to students across Bangladesh who are 13 years old or younger, born on or after October 1, 2013, and enrolled in Grade 6 or lower as of October 1, 2026. Students in the British curriculum (or similar streams) who are promoted to Grade 7 in July/August 2026 are not eligible.

What is the format of the Olympiad?

The BdMSO National Olympiad is a Math + Science exam with 60 minutes allocated per subject (50 + 50 = 100 marks total). It is held at multiple regional venues, simultaneously or on different dates. The day includes the National Anthem, the exam, a lunch break, a cultural program, and a prize-giving ceremony.

How do I register and what is the fee?

Registration is online at www.bdmso.org. The Olympiad registration fee is 1,000 BDT per subject, or 1,500 BDT for both Math and Science. Subsequent stages have separate fees: National Residential Camp 3,000–5,000 BDT, TST 1,000 BDT per subject, and the international participation fee is announced after the TST.

What prizes are awarded?

Approximately the top 10% of participants are designated Winners and receive a medal and Certificate of Achievement. Winners are classified as All-Round Winners (top 10% in both Mathematics and Science independently), Mathematics Winners (top 10% in Mathematics), or Science Winners (top 10% in Science). A Champion Trophy is awarded to the highest scorer in each section, presented on the final day of the National Residential Camp.

Every participant also receives a Participant T-shirt, Certificate of Participation, ID, Badge, Sticker, and Snacks. Lunch is provided to participants at the Dhaka and Chattogram venues only.

Is financial aid available?

Yes. The organizing committee may consider partial waivers of up to 50% on Olympiad, Camp, or TST fees for gifted students with significant financial need. Independent sponsorship is also encouraged.

How are IMSO team members selected?

Olympiad winners are invited to a three-day National Residential Camp. About a week after the camp, the Team Selection Test (TST) - a day-long evaluation per subject - is held. The primary National Team is determined by the top six students per subject based on a weighted average of their National Round, Camp performance, and TST scores. The 12 students (6 Math + 6 Science) plus 2 Tutors and 1 Team Leader form the standard 15-member delegation.

What is IMSO and how does BdMSO connect to it?

IMSO (International Mathematics and Science Olympiad) is a prestigious annual competition for primary school students under 13 from around the world. BdMSO is Bangladesh's official national qualifying round for IMSO. The national team selected through BdMSO will represent Bangladesh at the 23rd IMSO in 2026.

What are the costs for IMSO participation?

Selected national delegates pay a registration fee covering travel, visa, accommodation, and local transport. One parent may accompany their child for an additional fee of approximately $650 USD payable directly to the IMSO organizers.

How should my child prepare?

Follow the official BdMSO syllabus. It is recommended to practice elementary-level creative problems and study IMSO sample questions and past papers. Elementary-level Mathematics and Science textbooks are also useful.

How can I get further assistance?

Email us at support@bdmso.org or visit our Facebook page at facebook.com/imsobd.
